$comment: PINNED tool manifest for a dotden release (issue 3-19). This file is the SINGLE
source of truth that makes bundled binaries reproducible per release: bump a
version + its per-target sha256 here, never anywhere else. `scripts/fetch-binaries.mjs`
reads it to download, checksum-verify, and lay chezmoi + git into resources/bin/
<platform>/<arch>/ exactly where src/main/foundation/tools.ts resolves them.
Why pin: two environments on the same dotden version MUST behave identically, so the
chezmoi (the dotfile engine dotden wraps) and git (the Sync transport) bytes are frozen
per release rather than 'whatever the build host had'. Checksums make a swapped/corrupted
download fail loud instead of silently shipping the wrong tool (never fail silently).
`platform`/`arch` keys are Node's process.platform / process.arch values — the same ones
tools.ts joins into bin/<platform>/<arch>/. `member` is the path INSIDE the downloaded
archive to extract; the chezmoi windows asset is a bare .exe (no archive), marked raw.
git is bundled via desktop/dugite-native — the portable, self-contained git GitHub
Desktop ships (Apache-2.0). dotden's GitTransport (src/main/foundation/git-transport.ts)
shells out to a real `git`, so a relocatable distribution with its own libexec/templates
is required, not a bare binary. `extract: tree` unpacks the archive (whose root IS the
git distribution: ./bin, ./cmd, ./libexec/git-core, ./templates, …) directly into
bin/<platform>/<arch>/git-dist/, preserving structure; tools.ts then finds the launcher
at git-dist/bin/git (POSIX) or git-dist/cmd/git.exe (Windows), where it can resolve its
own support files. `launcher` is the post-extraction relative path asserted executable
by the fetch script (a smoke that the tree landed runnable).
